summarization.a: The line chart shows the mole fraction of HF and TMA at different positions along the x-axis.
summarization.b: The line chart displays the mole fraction of HF and TMA with sharp transitions at specific positions related to the tool geometry.
summarization.c: The line chart shows the mole fraction of HF and TMA with sharp transitions at specific positions related to the tool geometry.
summarization.d: The line chart illustrates the mole fraction of HF and TMA with sharp transitions at specific positions related to the tool geometry.

c.question: What is the width of the precursor slits?
c.answer: c	The width of the slits is equal to the width of the plateau, which is about 30 mm.

b.question: Why is the transition at the edge not perfectly straight?
b.answer: b	Part of the gases penetrates into the gas shield regions, which is why there is no precise cut-off.

a.question: If a gap distance of 10 mm is used, will there be ALE?
a.answer: a	No there will not be ALE, as TMA and HF already mix in the gas phase. This can result in continuous etching.

d.question: Would decreasing the gap size even further have an added benefit?
d.answer: d	Low gap sizes usually mean that lower flows of precursor are required so there will be less gas consumption.
